_comment: THE shared accept/reject corpus for the vendor contact-email predicate (#369).
frontend/src/lib/contact-email.test.ts, so a case added here is pinned on both sides and
cannot silently drift. Hand-maintained parallel lists were a review finding on the first
pass: they were already unequal at introduction.
Invisible characters are \uXXXX escapes ON PURPOSE - a literal one is unreviewable.
They exist because .NET's \s and JS's \s are DIFFERENT character classes (.NET's includes
U+0085 and excludes U+FEFF; JS's is the reverse), and Trim() vs .trim() diverge on the same
two code points. Both implementations therefore spell the blank class out explicitly instead
of relying on \s - these cases are what proves it. U+0000 additionally cannot be stored by
Postgres at all, so admitting it would 500 rather than 400.
